notes: Base URL https://api.stripe.com; all resource paths carry the /v1 prefix. The stripe adaptor is a thin generic wrapper: list(resource, options) -> GET /v1/{resource}, get(resource, id) -> GET /v1/{resource}/{id}, plus an http namespace (http.get, http.post, http.request) for arbitrary calls. It has no per-resource operations of its own, so the endpoint surface is defined by the resources it targets. The adaptor's documented examples use payment_intents, customers, charges and invoices; this spec covers list/retrieve/create for those four resources (11 operations). Focused subset extracted from Stripe's official OpenAPI 3.0 spec (stripe/openapi spec3.json), which is multiple megabytes and was NOT vendored whole. Resource schemas are trimmed to their commonly-used top-level fields (Stripe's full objects carry many deeply nested expandable sub-resources); metadata/address/shipping left as open objects. Stripe list responses use the standard {object:'list', url, has_more, data:[...]} envelope. Create endpoints accept application/x-www-form-urlencoded per the Stripe API.
